prompt: A hyper-realistic surrealist oil painting in the style of René Magritte. In the center of an empty, dreamlike desert under a twilight sky, there is a wooden dining chair. The seat and backrest of the chair have warped and splintered into an absurdly intricate, jagged, hyper-detailed negative mold. The mold perfectly captures the exact shape of a man's coat wrinkles, the loose change in his pocket, and a dropped pocket watch. Standing next to the chair is a featureless, smooth wooden mannequin, holding its arms out in confusion, completely unable to sit in the hyper-specific, jagged chair. Sharp, dramatic shadows and pristine, eerie lighting. <...> The details on the front of the suit should not be visible, nor should the watch or coins. Only their outlines should be visible in the wood/fabric of the chair, capturing the shape of the clothes of the man who previously sat there. Remove the signature. Keep all else the same.
tradition_approach: Ordinary, familiar objects in uncanny, illogical contexts with shifted scales. Depict things that are present, but unseen, playing with viewer's perception of what's hidden behind what's visible.
